sources.issuing_organisation: American Diabetes Association Professional Practice Committee for Diabetes
sources.exact_document_title: 9. Pharmacologic Approaches to Glycemic Treatment: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026
sources.jurisdiction: International guideline published by a United States professional organisation
sources.population: People with diabetes receiving clinician-led pharmacological treatment, with diabetes-type, age, pregnancy, comorbidity and treatment-specific qualifiers.
sources.clinical_setting: Diabetes medication selection, review, intensification, insulin initiation and treatment-plan follow-up.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for documenting person-centred medication review, glycaemic and weight goals, comorbidities, adverse effects, hypoglycaemia, treatment burden, preferences and clinician-decided insulin-initiation context. No medicine, dose or change is mapped.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 9.2–9.19 and Choice of Glucose-Lowering Therapy — person-centred treatment review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting glycaemic and weight goals, comorbidities, adverse effects, hypoglycaemia, treatment burden, adherence or access, preferences and continuing clinician review without selecting a medicine.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 9.20–9.30 and insulin-treatment discussion
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented severe hyperglycaemia context, insulin discussion, monitoring, education, treatment burden and follow-up when insulin is being considered or used. All product, route and dose content is excluded.

archive.publisher: American Diabetes Association Professional Practice Committee for Diabetes

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendations 9.2–9.19 and Choice of Glucose-Lowering Therapy — person-centred treatment review
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting glycaemic and weight goals, comorbidities, adverse effects, hypoglycaemia, treatment burden, adherence or access, preferences and continuing clinician review without selecting a medicine.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendations 9.20–9.30 and insulin-treatment discussion
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented severe hyperglycaemia context, insulin discussion, monitoring, education, treatment burden and follow-up when insulin is being considered or used. All product, route and dose content is excluded.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Type 2 Diabetes – 30
sources.population: Adults with type 2 diabetes; specific diagnostic exclusions are stated for children, pregnancy, and selected conditions.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for adult Dubai telehealth type 2 diabetes review; partial because the baseline workflow has no explicit adult-only filter and is not limited to telehealth.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines DHA-licensed telehealth type 2 diabetes scope and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

exact_sections.heading: 5.6 Annual/6 monthly review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies hypoglycaemia awareness, home glucose review, BP and BMI, renal testing, urine ACR, HbA1c, cholesterol, podiatry, and retinal review.

exact_sections.heading: 6. Referral Criteria and Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists metabolic decompensation, DKA or hyperosmolar state, infected foot ulcer, profound hypoglycaemia, and rapidly deteriorating complications.

exact_sections.heading: 7.2 Refilling/Prescribing Diabetic Medication — monitoring prerequisites
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies clinician review of BP, creatinine, electrolytes, HbA1c, cholesterol, urine ACR, home glucose context, and adherence before medication decisions.

exact_sections.heading: Appendix 7 — summary of required laboratory tests
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Summarises HbA1c, renal, lipid, ALT, and urine ACR review intervals for diabetes.
